frameworks.name: information theory (KL divergence between prior and posterior beliefs)
frameworks.one_line: pupil dilation indexes Bayesian belief update size during cognitive processing

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / decision making
frameworks.one_line: summed KL divergences over the accumulation process explain uncertainty-related dilation

frameworks.name: arousal modulation of pupil
frameworks.one_line: pupil as classical index of cognitive arousal under constant illumination

core_question: Can a single information-theoretic quantity unify the diverse cognitive correlates of pupil dilation?
key_assumptions: illumination is held constant so pupil reflects cognition not light
the brain maintains and updates an internal probabilistic model
belief updating can be quantified by KL divergence (prior vs posterior)
manipulations: stimulus informativeness about other task variables
key_findings: pupil response scales with stimulus self-information
pupil response scales with information the stimulus provides about other task variables
uncertainty-related dilation tracks summed KL divergences across the accumulation process
interpretations: diverse cognitive correlates of pupil dilation share a common denominator: KL-divergence belief updates
pupil-uncertainty link arises from wandering of evidence accumulation producing larger cumulative belief updates
